disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound morning stiffness of the shoulder and hip girdles lasting most of the day (well over 45-60 minutes), not just brief stiffness on waking.
trigger_question_examples: How long does the stiffness last after she wakes up — minutes, or most of the day?
Is she stiffest in the mornings, and roughly how long before it eases?
Does the stiffness improve as the day goes on, or stay all day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Mornings are the worst by a mile. It's not like it loosens off after a few minutes — she's been stiff and locked pretty much all day today.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: She's just sore all over really.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetrical proximal (shoulder and hip girdle) distribution with sparing of the small joints of the hands and feet — no swelling or pain in the fingers, wrists or toes.
trigger_question_examples: Are her hands, fingers or wrists sore or swollen at all, or is it just the shoulders and hips?
Does it affect the small joints of her hands and feet, or mainly the big muscle areas?
Any swelling or pain in the fingers or toes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, her hands and fingers are fine — no swelling there. It's really just the big muscles around her shoulders and her hips, both sides the same.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's mainly the shoulders and hips.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Giant cell arteritis screen NEGATIVE — no new or severe headache, no scalp tenderness (e.g. combing hair), no jaw pain/ache on chewing, and no visual disturbance (blurring, double vision, transient loss).
trigger_question_examples: Has she had any new headaches, tenderness of the scalp, or pain in her jaw when chewing?
Any changes to her vision — blurring, double vision, or losing sight even briefly?
Does her scalp hurt to touch, or does her jaw ache when she eats?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — no headaches, her scalp's fine, no jaw pain when she eats, and her vision's completely normal. It really is just the shoulders and hips.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think so, she hasn't mentioned anything about her head or eyes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid onset over ~48 hours with profound constitutional fatigue accompanying the stiffness.
trigger_question_examples: When exactly did this start and how quickly did it come on?
Has she been feeling tired or run down as well as stiff?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on fast — a dull ache two days ago, then it just escalated. And she's shattered, no energy at all, which isn't like her.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No distal neurological symptoms — no arm or leg weakness, no numbness or pins-and-needles, no shooting pain down the limbs, and no loss of bladder or bowel control.
trigger_question_examples: Does she have any weakness, numbness or pins-and-needles in her arms or legs?
Any shooting pains down her arms or legs, or any trouble controlling her bladder or bowels?
Can she feel and move her hands and feet normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, she can feel and move her hands and feet fine — no numbness or tingling, no shooting pains, and no problems with the toilet in that sense. It's the stiffness and pain stopping her, not weakness.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: She just can't move because it hurts so much.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe functional immobilisation with high falls risk — bedbound, two-person assist to sit, reliant on adult child for mobility, hydration and toileting.
trigger_question_examples: How is she managing day-to-day — can she get out of bed, walk, get to the toilet?
Is there anyone able to help her at home, and how much help does she need?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She can't do any of it on her own right now — she needs two of us just to sit her up, and she can't get to the toilet or get a drink without help. That's why I'm here.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Simple analgesia (paracetamol) has been tried with no effect on the stiffness or pain.
trigger_question_examples: Has she taken anything for it, and did it help?
What have you tried so far — any painkillers?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's had a couple of paracetamol but it did nothing at all — no difference.
